title: France: Picardy, Champagne & Burgundy (Laon, Troyes, Vézelay, Autun, St Jean de Vaux)

main_route.excerpt: 6 of us set off at 8.40. to listen to Peter play in an IAPS concert at Snape.

main_route.original_wording: Dover (The Nook campsite, twenty minutes from Dover)
main_route.excerpt: We motored down to The Nook *** ... A neat campsite, full of old people where we left Peter's case + viola. Twenty minutes from Dover. We left at 8.40. + caught the ferry before ours.

main_route.inference_reason: Calais is the named French entry port on the trip's hand-drawn route map (A_0136_R); the diary narrative describes catching the ferry from near Dover.
main_route.excerpt: [hand-drawn map of France showing route, labelled: Calais, St. Omer, Laon, Paris., Troyes, Bar sur Aube, Clairvaux, Vezelay, Morvan, Autun, Chalon-s-Saone]
[hand-drawn map of France showing route, labelled: Calais, St. Omer, Laon ...]

main_route.original_wording: Camp Hambouque, 11 km away from Guincourt; Pontavert
main_route.excerpt: We have come to a camp site with badminton, swings + table tennis. Camp Hambouque 11 km away from Guincourt. It's clean + has a washing machine. ... Pontavert
We set off in the rain to go to Laon. We took a funicular up to the town ... The cathedral is stately, tall columns, lovely windows.

main_route.normalized_name: Saint-Lyé / Saint-Remy area near Troyes, France
main_route.original_wording: St. Remy/Noue (on the old road from Arcis - Troyes)
main_route.excerpt: We had hoped to canoe in a campsite at Arcis sur Aube, but it was shut + overcrowded, so we lunched there + drove south to St. Remy on the old road from Arcis - Troyes. It's a green, quiet campsite
We walked around Troyes. The cathedral is lovely, calm, uncluttered. Stained glass windows. Troyes grew rich from being on the Italy/Belgium trade route.
On to [?Arcy]-sur-[?Cure]**** There is a fast flowing river, where we had a good time swimmin[g]
We are driving south through Morvin + had lunch outside Autun. We parked + walked to Autun Cathedral, to see more glorious carving

main_route.original_wording: St. Jean de Vaux (Rue des Sources)
main_route.excerpt: We drove on to St. Jean de Vaux, & camped in the middle of the Burgundy Vineyards. We got there just before the pool shut

main_route.original_wording: Bar (old town, municipal campsite); Bar sur Aube
main_route.excerpt: We came into an old town, Bar-[illegible] to camp in the municipal campsite. We had stopped at Clairvaux on the way ... We walked out into Bar for supper. It was delicious, in the Cellier des Moines.
We drove north + looked at a wood + plaster church at Ste. Marie-du-lac, a nice church with a minstrel's gallery.
We reached humbres at 7.15. The gate was shut from 7-9 ... (final legible page; return to Birmingham implied as trip end).

crossings.excerpt: Twenty minutes from Dover. We left at 8.40. + caught the ferry before ours. ... [route map A_0136_R names Calais as the French port]

excursions.label: Chemin des Dames day visits (Grotte de Sagon & Vauclair Abbey)

stops.normalized_name: Grotte du Dragon (Caverne du Dragon), Chemin des Dames, France
stops.original_wording: Grotte de Sagon on the Chemin des Dames
stops.excerpt: We drove to Grotte de Sagon on the Chemin des Dames where 6,000 German soldiers held out in 1914-7 war. ... We saw a museum + film
On to Vauclair Abbey. In parts you can imagine the rooms. ... Accanthus capitol, similar to Laon, at Cistercian monastery, Vauclair on Chemin des Dames
We reached Vézelay. It's on a hill. Glorious. 5 different carvers. ... It was the start of pilgrimages to Compostella.

excursions.label: Day trips to Chalon-sur-Saône & Givry (wine country)

stops.excerpt: we'd go to Chalon sur Saone if the storm hadn't stopped. ... We window-shopped around the town & had lunch in a very, very friendly restaurant

stops.original_wording: Givry (Restaurant de Raison, Hotel de Garage)
stops.excerpt: We went out in the van in the evening to Restaurant de Raison, Hotel de Garage, Givry, where we went last year & again had a good meal.

excursions.label: Stop at Clairvaux en route to Bar-sur-Aube

stops.excerpt: We had stopped at Clairvaux on the way, which was disappointing because tours were only allowed on Saturdays

unresolved.issue: Place name uncertain and unlocatable; described as 'a family outing place' visited after the ferry on the first day. Could be a campsite/leisure park name rather than a town. Cannot place on the map.
unresolved.excerpt: Then we drove on to [?Suny] ***, a family outing place. Men were playing boules all the time. There was mini golf + table football.

unresolved.issue: Place name uncertain; likely the village by the lake near Arcy-sur-Cure where the Vézelay-area campsite was. Possibly Vermenton or similar; spelling illegible. Treated as the seq 10 base region (Arcy-sur-Cure) but the exact village name is unresolved.
unresolved.excerpt: we came back along the winding road to [?hormes]. * * * * The campsite is at the end of a long, ugly road away from the village

unresolved.issue: Final overnight stop name uncertain/illegible ('We reached humbres at 7.15'). Likely a French campsite on the homeward leg rather than Birmingham; the diary record ends mid-page (boundary_mixed) before the actual return home is narrated. Recorded as trip-end but the true name and whether it is the last French night or a UK stop is unresolved.
unresolved.excerpt: We reached humbres at 7.15. The gate was shut from 7-9 for the accent to go and eat supper. It's open 7am - 7pm + 9-10pm.

boundary_flags.issue: boundary_mixed: page opens with the tail of a previous outing (Thorpeness holiday with Annie & Klaus) before this trip's 'Sat July 30th' start.
boundary_mixed: final page of the trip; narrative ends mid-entry at 'humbres' without an explicit arrival home, so date_end and the return leg are approximate.
Date transcription anomaly: heading 'Wed Aug 2nd' carries iso 1995-08-02 in the bundle, but context (1994 trip, sequence after Tues Aug 2nd) indicates 1994-08-03. Treated as 1994.

summary: A summer 1994 family camping tour of northern and eastern France, drawn on a hand-sketched route map running Calais - St. Omer - Laon - Troyes - Vézelay - Morvan - Autun - Chalon-sur-Saône, with a return branch via Bar-sur-Aube and Clairvaux. After a concert at Snape and a night near Dover, the family crossed Dover-Calais and worked south through Picardy (Laon, the Chemin des Dames battlefields and Vauclair Abbey), Champagne (Troyes), and Burgundy, basing at campsites near Pontavert, Troyes, Arcy-sur-Cure (for Vézelay), and St-Jean-de-Vaux in the vineyards (revisiting Givry from the previous year). They returned north via Bar-sur-Aube, Clairvaux and Ste-Marie-du-Lac before the homeward leg.
extraction_notes: Route ORDER follows the hand-drawn France map on A_0136_R (Calais, St. Omer, Laon, Paris, Troyes, Bar sur Aube, Clairvaux, Vezelay, Morvan, Autun, Chalon-s-Saone), reconciled with the dated narrative for overnight progression. Paris appears only as a labelled dot west of the dotted route line on the sketch and is NOT narrated as visited, so it is intentionally omitted as a stop. The sketch groups Bar-sur-Aube/Clairvaux geographically near Troyes, but the dated narrative places them on the NORTHWARD return leg (Aug 10) after Burgundy; main_route order follows the chronological overnight chain. Morvan is a region (the Morvan massif) the family drove through, not a discrete stop, so it is folded into the Autun leg rather than listed separately. The Dover-Calais crossing is treated as named (Calais on the sketch map; Dover in the narrative) hence inferred=false. The first-day '[?Suny]' family outing place, the '[?hormes]' village by Arcy-sur-Cure, and the final 'humbres' overnight are all illegible/uncertain and listed under unresolved. The two bee-museum visits (southbound near Arcis and northbound near Chalons-sur-Marne) and the Restaurant des Grottes are activity stops, not route nodes. The trip likely continued home after A_0138_L; pages end mid-trip so the explicit return to Birmingham is inferred (low confidence).
